question: [File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting shard.realm.evmAddress format strings where the EVM address portion is a valid opaque address (non-zero prefix), forcing getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to execute a database query on every request since opaque EVM addresses cannot be resolved locally and always require a DB round-trip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting realm.alias format strings (two-part alias with realm prefix) that pass AccountAlias.isValid() and trigger AccountAlias.fromString() parsing followed by a database query, with no caching of negative results, allowing sustained database load from repeated non-existent alias lookups?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that getAccountFromAlias() creates a new Entity object (new Entity(rows[0])) on every successful lookup, which uses lodash mapKeys and camelCase transformation on all entity columns, causing excessive CPU usage under high request volume due to repeated object transformation overhead?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting inputs that fail both EntityId.isValidEntityId() and AccountAlias.isValid() checks, causing getEncodedId() to throw InvalidArgumentError.forParams() on every request, and under high volume this generates excessive error object creation and stack trace generation overhead that degrades service throughput?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting EVM addresses with the 0x prefix that are valid per evmAddressRegex, since getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() calls Buffer.from(entityId.evmAddress, 'hex') where evmAddress is stored without the 0x prefix after parsing, and flooding with unique addresses forces repeated DB queries with no caching at the service layer?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isValidAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by flooding the API with requests that trigger isValidAccount() with sequential or random accountId values, since getSingleRow() executes entityExistenceQuery for every call with no caching, and the absence of any rate limiting allows the attacker to saturate the database with existence-check queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting shard.realm.num format entity IDs where the num component is at the maximum 12-digit boundary, forcing BigInt() conversion and boundary checks in parseFromString() on every request, and under high concurrency causing CPU saturation from repeated BigInt arithmetic operations?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting alias strings at the maximum length allowed by accountAliasRegex (up to 5-digit shard, 5-digit realm, and maximum base32 characters), forcing maximum-length base32.decode() operations on every request with no caching, leading to sustained CPU and memory pressure?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that the EntityId LRU cache (parseCached) is keyed on the string representation of the input plus flags, and by submitting inputs with unique shard.realm.num combinations that all resolve to different cache keys, the attacker can fill the cache to maxSize, causing memory pressure and eviction of legitimate cached entries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting EVM addresses in the shard.realm.evmAddress format where shard and realm match the system configuration, forcing the service to always take the database lookup path (since the address is opaque), and flooding with unique addresses to exhaust database connections and query capacity?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountIdFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the requireResult=true default in getAccountIdFromAlias(), where every failed alias lookup throws a NotFoundError that includes error object creation and stack trace generation, and under high volume this creates excessive garbage collection pressure from short-lived error objects, degrading service throughput?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting negative encoded entity IDs (valid per encodedEntityIdRegex which allows a leading minus sign), forcing parseFromEncodedId() to perform BigInt() conversion and boundary checks including BigInt.asIntN() operations on every request, leading to CPU exhaustion under high concurrency?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting alias strings that are valid per accountAliasRegex but decode to binary values that cause the database to perform expensive bytea comparisons against the alias column, and by flooding with many unique such aliases, the attacker can saturate database I/O and CPU without any server-side throttling?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the sequential evaluation in getEncodedId() where EntityId.isValidEntityId() is checked first and AccountAlias.isValid() second, and by crafting inputs that fail the first check but pass the second, forcing the service to always execute both regex evaluations plus a database query per request, maximizing per-request CPU cost?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that entityFromEvmAddressQuery uses a bytea parameter ($1 = Buffer.from(entityId.evmAddress, 'hex')), and flooding with unique 20-byte EVM address values forces the database to perform repeated bytea index lookups, saturating database I/O and causing query latency to spike for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting inputs that are valid EVM addresses (40 hex chars) but also match the evmAddressShardRealmRegex with a realm prefix, causing EntityId.parseString() to attempt shard/realm validation and then fall through to a database lookup, with no caching of the negative result, allowing sustained database load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that getAccountFromAlias() passes accountAlias.alias (a decoded binary Buffer from base32.decode) directly as a query parameter, and by crafting aliases that decode to binary patterns causing expensive database index lookups, the attacker can maximize per-query database cost?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: isValidAccount()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by flooding the API with isValidAccount() calls using BigInt accountId values at the boundary of JavaScript's safe integer range, forcing the database to handle large integer parameters and potentially causing query plan changes that degrade performance for all concurrent users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting realm.num format entity IDs (two-part format) at high volume, since these pass EntityId.isValidEntityId() and are resolved locally without a DB query but still require regex matching, BigInt conversion, and LRU cache operations on every request, causing CPU saturation under high concurrency?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the singleton export pattern (export default new EntityService()), where the single shared EntityService instance handles all concurrent requests, and by flooding with alias lookups the attacker can saturate the shared database connection pool used by this singleton, blocking all other service methods that share the same pool?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting shard.realm.alias format strings where the alias portion is at maximum valid length, forcing AccountAlias.fromString() to split the string, call base32.decode() on the maximum-length alias component, and then execute a database query, maximizing per-request CPU and I/O cost with no caching?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() is called from getEncodedId() only when entityId.evmAddress !== null (opaque EVM address path), and by flooding with unique opaque EVM addresses the attacker forces a database query on every single request with no result caching at the service layer?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by exploiting the fact that the entityFromAliasQuery selects only Entity.ID but getAccountFromAlias() wraps the result in new Entity(rows[0]) which calls mapKeys with camelCase transformation on all returned columns, and by flooding with matching alias lookups the attacker causes repeated lodash object transformation overhead?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting inputs that are exactly at the boundary between EntityId.isValidEntityId() returning true and false, requiring the regex engine to backtrack maximally before determining validity, and under high request volume causing CPU exhaustion from repeated worst-case regex evaluation?

[File: 'File Name: rest/service/entityService.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a non-network DoS by submitting inputs that pass AccountAlias.isValid() (matching accountAliasRegex /^(\\d{1,5}\\.){0,2}[A-Z2-7]+$/) with maximum-length digit prefixes and maximum-length base32 suffix, forcing the regex engine to perform maximum-length matching on every request and causing CPU saturation under high concurrency?
